marketSize_JOD.note: Jordan Restaurant Association 2024: F&B is one of the largest SME sectors. Talabat Jordan processes 500,000+ monthly orders. Amman cafe market growing 8-12% annually driven by young population and social cafe culture.
marketSize_JOD.aiPromptGuidance: For cafe or coffee shop ideas use cafesAndCoffeeShops_amman. For sandwich shops or fast food use fastFoodAndSandwiches_amman. For cloud kitchens use cloudKitchens_jordan. Never use total F&B figure for a single small cafe — realistic addressable market for one neighborhood cafe is 0.5-2M JOD.

cafe_coffee.name: Books@Cafe
cafe_coffee.type: Iconic Amman cafe with books and cultural events
cafe_coffee.mainStrength: Iconic brand, loyal repeat customers, unique Rainbow Street location, cultural identity

cafe_coffee.name: Rumi Cafe
cafe_coffee.mainStrength: Strong Instagram presence, specialty coffee quality, loyal community following

cafe_coffee.name: Caribou Coffee
cafe_coffee.locations: Multiple Amman branches including malls and commercial areas
cafe_coffee.mainStrength: International brand trust, consistent quality, loyalty app, drive-through locations

cafe_coffee.name: Independent Neighborhood Cafes (archetype)
cafe_coffee.type: Small local cafes in residential Amman areas
cafe_coffee.locations: Sweifieh, Khalda, Jubeiha, Medina Street, Tlaa Al-Ali
cafe_coffee.mainStrength: Lower prices, convenience, familiar local atmosphere, daily habit customers
cafe_coffee.note: Hundreds of small independent cafes in every Amman neighborhood. This is the most direct competitor for any new small cafe.

fast_food_sandwiches.name: Hashem Restaurant
fast_food_sandwiches.targetSegment: All demographics — tourist and local institution
fast_food_sandwiches.mainStrength: Legendary status, ultra-low prices, 60+ years brand equity

fast_food_sandwiches.name: Shawarma Reem
fast_food_sandwiches.mainStrength: Consistent quality, fast service, loyal daily customers, multiple locations

delivery_platforms.name: Talabat
delivery_platforms.mainStrength: #1 food delivery in Jordan, massive customer base, marketing exposure for new restaurants
delivery_platforms.note: Talabat is a channel AND a competitor. It provides traffic but takes 20-30% commission per order. A 5 JOD sandwich that costs 2 JOD to make yields only 1.0-1.5 JOD profit after Talabat commission.

marketContext.cafeCulture: Amman has one of the highest cafe-per-capita rates in the Arab world. Cafe-going is a primary social activity for youth.
marketContext.socialMediaImpact: A well-photographed cafe interior can generate 10,000+ Instagram impressions in first week through local food bloggers. Aesthetics drive trial visits.
marketContext.corporateBreakfast: B2B catering for office breakfast is underserved in Amman — opportunity for cafes near business districts

legalRequirements.municipalityLicense_JOD: 500-1500 depending on Amman district and space size
legalRequirements.processingTime_weeks: 4-8 weeks for full licensing. Plan for delays.
legalRequirements.note: Cannot operate without municipality license. Apply immediately — delays are common and expensive.

small_cafe_60_80sqm.rent_jubeiha_note: Jubeiha and east Amman residential areas: 600-900 JOD/month for similar size
small_cafe_60_80sqm.note: West Amman (Sweifieh, Khalda) rent is 1000-1800 JOD/month for 60-80 sqm commercial in 2025. Jubeiha and residential areas are 500-900 JOD/month.

cafe_beverages.note: Coffee beverages have high margins. A 3 JOD cappuccino costs ~0.8 JOD to make = 73% gross margin.

food_sandwiches.note: Sandwiches and food items have lower margins due to ingredients and labor.

blended_cafe_food.note: Blended margin for a cafe selling both beverages and food items.

talabat_adjusted_margin.note: After Talabat 20-30% commission, gross margin drops significantly. Heavy Talabat dependence destroys profitability.

daily_revenue_needed_to_cover_costs.note: A small cafe with 2500 JOD/month costs needs 83 JOD/day minimum at 30-day month. Target 100-150 JOD/day to build profit.

talabat_listing.note: No upfront cost but 20-30% ongoing commission per order

googleMaps.note: Free listing — essential for any physical cafe

talabat_customers.note: Delivery customers are much less loyal than walk-in regulars

breakEven_months.note: Cafes in Amman typically take 8-14 months to reach full break-even. Budget must cover fixed costs for this entire period.

commonRisks: Sweifieh and west Amman cafe market is extremely saturated — dozens of established competitors within walking distance
Talabat commissions of 20-30% destroy margins on delivery orders — use Talabat for discovery, convert to WhatsApp for repeat orders
Municipality licensing delays can push opening back 6-12 weeks — apply immediately
Commercial espresso machine breakdown can stop operations — budget 500-1000 JOD for emergency maintenance
Staff turnover: baristas and cooks frequently leave for better-paying positions — train backup staff
Ramadan shifts all customer patterns: evening business spikes, morning business collapses
Word of mouth takes 3-6 months to build in a new neighborhood — cash reserve must cover this gap

redFlags.note: If Talabat orders represent more than 60% of revenue, the business is paying 20-30% commission on majority of income — not sustainable.

jordanContext.primaryAcquisitionChannel: Instagram aesthetics + Google Maps reviews + Talabat listing for discovery
jordanContext.talabatStrategy: List on Talabat at launch for visibility, but actively move repeat customers to WhatsApp ordering with a loyalty discount. Reduces commission dependency over time.
jordanContext.cafeAesthetics: Instagram-worthy interior is now a business requirement in Amman, not a luxury. Budget 2000-5000 JOD for interior design that photographs well.
jordanContext.jubeihaOpportunity: Jubeiha and university areas have lower competition and lower rent than west Amman, with a large captive student population. Strong opportunity for budget cafes.
jordanContext.sweifiehWarning: Sweifieh is one of the most cafes-per-block areas in Amman. New entrants need a clear differentiator — specialty product, unique design, or lower price — to survive.
